Activity or Task:
Activity 1: Setting up HTC Vive Base Stations on Tripods
1. Transport base stations and tripods to the VXLab or testing area.
Hazards & Risks: Manual Handling: Ergonomic from repeated bending/lifting/carrying equipment.
Current Risk Controls: Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Prefer using equipment trolley to transport multiple tripods and base stations.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 1: Setting up HTC Vive Base Stations on Tripods
2. Extend tripod legs and position tripods at designated locations around the tracking area.
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Tripod legs creating tripping hazard for operators and bystanders.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Mark tripod leg positions with visible tape or cones on the floor. Ensure tripod legs are not placed in walkways or thoroughfares. Retract tripod legs when not in active use.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 1: Setting up HTC Vive Base Stations on Tripods
3. Mount base stations onto tripods using the 1/4" mounting thread and clamping ring.
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Dropping the base station during mounting; pinch hazard from clamping ring. Equipment Damage: Base station falling and shattering if dropped.
Current Risk Controls: Follow mounting instructions in the base station user guide.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure tripod is correctly set up and stable before mounting the base station. Mount base stations at or below eye level where practical.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 1: Setting up HTC Vive Base Stations on Tripods
4. Adjust base station angle (loosening clamping ring while holding the station).
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Dropping the base station during adjustment; pinch hazard from clamping ring. Equipment Damage: Base station falling and shattering.
Current Risk Controls: Follow angle adjustment instructions in the base station user guide.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure the clamping ring is fully tightened before releasing grip on the base station. Use two hands when adjusting; one supporting the station, one operating the ring.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 1: Setting up HTC Vive Base Stations on Tripods
5. Connect power cables and plug adapters into power outlets.
Hazards & Risks: Electrical: Electric shock; fire risk from damaged cables. Trip Hazard: Power cables across floor.
Current Risk Controls: Use only supplied power cables and adapters. Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Route power cables along tripod legs and use cable covers or tape to secure cables to the floor. Position tripods near power outlets to minimise cable run length. Inspect cables for damage before each use.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 1: Setting up HTC Vive Base Stations on Tripods
6. Power on base stations and verify operational status (green LED).
Hazards & Risks: Electrical: Electric shock from damaged units.
Current Risk Controls: Inspect base station front panel for cracks or damage before powering on.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Visually inspect the base station unit and its power cable for cracks, damage, fraying, or exposed wiring before powering on. If any damage is found, tag the base station unit with a "Do Not Use" label and report it to the supervisor. Test the power cable for continuity with a multimeter if damage is suspected.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
1. Connect dongle to dongle cradle via USB cable, and connect the other end of the USB cable to the computer.
Hazards & Risks: Trip Hazard: USB cable routed across workspace.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Route USB cable along desk edges or use cable clips. Keep cable length as short as practical. Place dongle cradle in a location where the cable does not cross walkways.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
2. Place dongle at least 45 cm (18 in) from the computer.
Hazards & Risks: RF Interference: Tracking dropout or degraded performance if dongle is too close to computer.
Current Risk Controls: Follow dongle placement instructions in the tracker user manual (>=45 cm from computer).
Additional Risk Control Measures: Mark the dongle cable at 45 cm from the dongle to indicate minimum distance. Ensure the dongle is placed on a stable surface where it will not be moved.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
3. Attach tracker to the Booster K1 robot using the 1/4" screw mounting mechanism and stabilizing pin.
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Pinch hazard from docking mechanism. Equipment Damage: Dropping the tracker; over-tightening causing damage; stripping threads.
Current Risk Controls: Follow tracker mounting instructions in the tracker user manual and v1 technical reference.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure the tracker is securely fastened before releasing grip. Do not over-tighten the 1/4" screw; tighten only until snug. Verify the stabilizing pin is fully engaged in its recess.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
4. Verify tracker placement does not obstruct robot sensors or movement.
Hazards & Risks: Equipment Damage: Accessory body obstructing robot sensors or limiting range of motion. Tracking Degradation: Accessory extending beyond recommended placing cone (270 deg FOV) blocking sensor view.
Current Risk Controls: Follow placement guidelines in the tracker v1 technical reference.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Visually inspect that the tracker's sensor dome has a clear line of sight to the base stations. Verify the tracker is within the 270 deg FOV cone. Test robot range of motion after mounting to ensure no obstruction.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
5. Power on tracker (briefly press Power button).
Hazards & Risks: Operational: Tracker may automatically turn off if not paired within 30 seconds.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure the dongle is connected to the PC and SteamVR is running before powering on the tracker. Proceed to pairing immediately after powering on to avoid the 30-second pairing timeout.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
6. Pair tracker with dongle via SteamVR app (press Power button for ~2 seconds).
Hazards & Risks: RF Interference: Potential RF interference in lab environment.
Current Risk Controls: Follow pairing instructions in the tracker user manual.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure the dongle is placed at least 45 cm from the PC and other wireless devices to minimise RF interference during pairing. Confirm the tracker appears in SteamVR before proceeding.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 2: Setting up HTC Vive Tracker and Dongle
7. Verify connection status (green LED = normal, solid green in SteamVR).
Hazards & Risks: Operational: Misinterpreting LED status could lead to operating with low battery or in pairing mode.
Current Risk Controls: Refer to LED status indicator table in tracker user manual.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Confirm the tracker LED shows solid green (normal mode) and the SteamVR interface shows the tracker icon as detected. If the LED shows blinking red (low battery), charge the tracker before use. If blinking blue, pairing is still in progress. If no LED, the tracker may be off or out of battery.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 3: Charging HTC Vive Trackers
1. Connect USB cable to tracker micro-USB port.
Hazards & Risks: Electrical: Electric shock if cable or port is damaged.
Current Risk Controls: Use only the supplied USB cable.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Visually inspect the USB cable for fraying, cuts, exposed wires, or bent connectors before each use. Check for physical damage such as kinks, crushed sections, or melted insulation. Perform a continuity test with a multimeter if damage is suspected. Monitor for unusual heat or smell during first use after any suspected damage.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 3: Charging HTC Vive Trackers
2. Connect USB cable to power adapter (5V +/-5%, 1000 mA) and plug into power outlet.
Hazards & Risks: Electrical: Overheating if using incorrect charger; fire risk.
Current Risk Controls: Use only the power adapter that came with VIVE controllers (5V +/-5%, 1000 mA). Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Do not leave charging trackers unattended for extended periods. Charge on a non-flammable surface away from combustible materials. Inspect charger and cable for damage before each use.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 3: Charging HTC Vive Trackers
3. Alternatively, connect USB cable to PC USB port (500 mA).
Hazards & Risks: Electrical: USB port overload if multiple devices connected.
Current Risk Controls: Follow charging instructions in the tracker user manual.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Limit the number of devices connected to the PC USB ports while charging. Use a powered USB hub instead of direct PC USB ports if necessary. Check USB port power limits before connecting devices.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 3: Charging HTC Vive Trackers
4. Monitor charging status (orange LED while charging; green/white when fully charged).
Hazards & Risks: Operational: Misinterpreting LED status leading to overcharging or operating with insufficient charge.
Current Risk Controls: Refer to LED status indicator table in tracker user manual.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Verify the status light is functioning by observing it change state when the tracker is connected to power (should show orange while charging). Confirm the LED changes to green/white when fully charged before disconnecting. If the status light does not illuminate or behave as expected, do not rely on it; use the SteamVR app to verify battery level instead.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 3: Charging HTC Vive Trackers
5. Disconnect USB cable once fully charged.
Hazards & Risks: Trip Hazard: USB cable left across workspace after disconnection.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Coil and store USB cable immediately after disconnection.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
1. Ensure the tracking area (play area) is entirely clear of obstacles, objects, and other individuals.
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Collision with obstacles or other individuals in the tracking space while the robot is in motion.
Current Risk Controls: Clear the play area before operation as per tracker user manual. Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Conduct a visual sweep of the tracking area before each session. Erect physical barriers or warning tape around the perimeter of the tracking area. Only permitted personnel may be within the tracking area during operation.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
2. Power on the Booster K1 robot (with tracker attached) according to the robot ARA.
Hazards & Risks: As defined in the Booster K1 ARA (AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development).
Current Risk Controls: Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
3. Launch the Vive ROS2 integration software on the PC to begin receiving and recording tracking data.
Hazards & Risks: Software: ROS2 node misconfiguration causing failure to record tracking data.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Verify that all base stations are powered on and trackers are detected (green LED) before launching ROS2. Confirm ROS2 topics are publishing data before proceeding with recording.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
4. Verify tracking data is being received correctly (check ROS2 topics for pose data).
Hazards & Risks: Data Loss: Incorrect or noisy tracking data leading to unusable recordings.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Monitor ROS2 tracking topics for data validity. Define acceptable bounds for tracking data (e.g., position, orientation). If tracking data is out of bounds, troubleshoot before continuing.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
5. Operate the robot (teleoperation or autonomous) according to the robot ARA while the tracking system records pose data.
Hazards & Risks: As defined in the Booster K1 ARA (AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development). The tracking system is passive and does not affect robot control.
Current Risk Controls: Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development. Hold and have ready access to the joystick for E-Stop. Always monitor the K1.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
6. Monitor for tracking degradation during operation (bright light, reflective surfaces).
Hazards & Risks: Data Loss: Reduced tracking accuracy due to bright light or reflective surfaces, resulting in poor-quality recordings.
Current Risk Controls: Be aware of environmental factors that affect tracking performance.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Position base stations away from direct bright light sources. Avoid reflective surfaces in the tracking area.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 4: Operating the HTC Vive Motion Tracking System (Vive ROS2 Integration)
7. Monitor tracker battery level during operation.
Hazards & Risks: Data Loss: Tracker automatically turning off due to low battery, pairing timeout (>30 s idle), or inactivity (>5 min no movement), causing loss of tracking data.
Current Risk Controls: Monitor tracker LED status (blinking red = low battery).
Additional Risk Control Measures: Check tracker battery level before each session. Keep spare charged trackers available for swap. If tracker turns off unexpectedly, the robot continues operating normally; only tracking data recording is affected.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 5: Updating Firmware (Base Stations and Trackers)
1. Connect base station to computer via micro-USB cable (for base station firmware update).
Hazards & Risks: Trip Hazard: USB cable across workspace.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Keep USB cable routed along desk edges. Ensure the cable is not across walkways.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 5: Updating Firmware (Base Stations and Trackers)
2. Connect tracker to computer via supplied USB cable (for tracker firmware update).
Hazards & Risks: Trip Hazard: USB cable across workspace.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Keep USB cable routed along desk edges. Ensure the cable is not across walkways.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 5: Updating Firmware (Base Stations and Trackers)
3. Initiate firmware update via SteamVR app (tracker) or Bluetooth/USB (base station).
Hazards & Risks: Equipment Damage: Bricking the device if power or USB is disconnected during update.
Current Risk Controls: Do not unplug the USB cable or disconnect power any time before the firmware update is complete. Follow firmware update instructions in the respective user guides.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure the computer has stable power (plugged in, not on battery). Do not put the computer to sleep during the update. Verify the correct firmware file before proceeding; confirm with the supervisor responsible for the equipment that the correct firmware version and file is being used. Allow the update to complete without interruption.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 5: Updating Firmware (Base Stations and Trackers)
4. Verify firmware update completed successfully (check device status).
Hazards & Risks: Equipment Damage: Incorrect firmware file causing device malfunction.
Current Risk Controls: Follow verification steps in the user guides.
Additional Risk Control Measures: After the update completes, power cycle the device and confirm it operates normally (e.g., LED status, detection in SteamVR). Report the successful update to the supervisor responsible for the equipment. If the device does not function as expected, tag it with a "Do Not Use" label and notify the supervisor immediately.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 6: Packing Up and Storing HTC Vive Equipment
1. Power off base stations and disconnect power cables.
Hazards & Risks: Electrical: Electric shock if cables are pulled forcefully.
Current Risk Controls: Power off before disconnecting.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure all cables and units are tagged and tested before operation. Visually inspect cables for fraying, cuts, or exposed wires. If damage is found, tag the affected unit with a "Do Not Use" label and report it to the supervisor.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 6: Packing Up and Storing HTC Vive Equipment
2. Unmount base stations from tripods.
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Dropping the base station during unmounting. Equipment Damage: Base station falling and shattering.
Current Risk Controls: Use two-person operation: one holds the tripod steady, the other unmounts the base station.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Ensure the base station is securely held before loosening the clamping ring. Place the base station in its protective case immediately after unmounting.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 6: Packing Up and Storing HTC Vive Equipment
3. Collapse tripod legs and pack tripods.
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Pinch hazard from collapsing tripod legs.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Collapse tripod legs one section at a time, keeping fingers clear of locking mechanisms.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 6: Packing Up and Storing HTC Vive Equipment
4. Remove tracker from Booster K1 robot (if applicable).
Hazards & Risks: Physical Injury: Pinch hazard from docking mechanism. Equipment Damage: Dropping the tracker.
Current Risk Controls: Follow tracker removal instructions.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Place the tracker in its protective case immediately after removal.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 6: Packing Up and Storing HTC Vive Equipment
5. Disconnect and store dongle, cables, and accessories.
Hazards & Risks: Trip Hazard: USB cable left across workspace after disconnection.
Current Risk Controls: None specific.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Coil and store all cables immediately after disconnection. Store dongle in its cradle or protective case.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.

Activity or Task:
Activity 6: Packing Up and Storing HTC Vive Equipment
6. Transport equipment to storage location.
Hazards & Risks: Manual Handling: Ergonomic from repeated bending/lifting/carrying equipment.
Current Risk Controls: Comply with the Booster P&ERA: AIIL-Booster-K1-PAR-Development. Comply with RMIT manual handling policies.
Additional Risk Control Measures: Use equipment trolley for multiple items. Do not carry more than one tripod at a time. Bend with knees and keep a straight back when lifting.
Responsibility: Dr. Timothy Wiley (Academic Supervisor). All operators and users.
